preview_text:
The model favours Algeria at 38%, with Austria trailing at 30% and a draw at 32%. This represents a relatively competitive three-way contest, though the model assigns the African side a meaningful edge over both alternatives.

The spread across outcomes reflects genuine uncertainty. No single result dominates; Algeria's 8-point advantage over Austria is modest enough that the underdog remains a live contender. A draw sits squarely between the two win probabilities, suggesting a match unlikely to be decided by dominant play. The evenness of the distribution points to a closely matched fixture.

A notable divergence emerges between the model and the sportsbook consensus. The market backs Austria substantially more, placing it at 44%—a 14-point gap from the model's 38% for Algeria. Polymarket prices align closely with the sportsbook view. The dashboard offers both estimates without adjudicating which interpretation of the matchup is more accurate.
